page: Amazon Web Services r8i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores based on the x86_64 Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512).

In benchmark evaluations, the r8i.16xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core performance, placing in the top tier for Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers high read-only bandwidth, scoring in the top 25% for both cached small-block and uncached large-block operations. The processor's AMX capabilities contribute to strong performance in low-latency workloads, including top-tier results in Redis SET operations, static web serving, and medium-parameter LLM prompt processing. However, text generation for large 70B models falls into the average tier due to the absence of dedicated GPU hardware.

This instance configuration does not include local NVMe storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 30 Gbps. The lack of bundled local storage represents a tradeoff for I/O-heavy database workloads that require ultra-low local latency, though the high-speed DDR5 memory and 30 Gbps network interface mitigate data transfer bottlenecks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8i.16xlarge offers a high density of high-speed DDR5 memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound enterprise applications without the overhead of GPU hardware. It is suited for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput Redis deployments, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a 480 MB L3 cache. The instance lacks local storage, relying instead on network-attached storage supported by a 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In performance benchmarks, the r8i.16xlarge excels in memory bandwidth, Redis operations, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing, placing in the top tier for Geekbench and Passmark suites. This hardware profile is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise application hosting, offering a balanced, high-density memory configuration without the added cost of dedicated GPU accelerators.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, designed for high-throughput database and in-memory workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density DDR5 memory instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and advanced matrix extensions for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 512 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory.
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 3.9 GHz clock speed and 480 MB L3 cache.
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix math.
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage.
Top-tier performance in Redis operations and software compilation.
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and CPU-based inference.
